t_ch: （该去大桥对面和佩拉会合了。）
t_en: (It's time to meet Pela across the bridge.)

t_ch: （附近似乎有怪物在游荡…小心前进吧。）
t_en: (There seem to be monsters roaming nearby... Let's proceed with caution.)

t_ch: （这个怪物似乎正把守着大门…想办法打倒它吧。）
t_en: (This monster seems to be guarding the gate... Find a way to take it down.)

t_ch: 喂——{NICKNAME}——你能听到吗——
t_en: Hey! {NICKNAME}! Can you hear me?

t_ch: （似乎听到了佩拉的呼喊……）
t_en: (I seem to hear Pela's voice...)

t_ch: 辛苦你了……
t_en: Thank you so much...

t_ch: 放弃解锁
t_en: Give it up.

t_ch: 画面上的男性身着铁卫旧制制服，看起来风度非凡——如果他没做鬼脸的话。
t_en: The man in the picture is dressed in the old uniform of the Guards and has quite a classy look about him — if only he weren't making such a silly face.

t_ch: 咦，这底下好像还有一句话…<color=#dbc291ff>「下一个线索在拱门的荫蔽中酣眠」</color>？
t_en: Um, there seems to be another sentence below... <color=#dbc291ff>"The next clue is sleeping in the shadow of the arch"</color>?

t_ch: 至于新的线索，大概是和这个<color=#dbc291ff>「拱门」</color>有关。我们就把它当做下一个目标吧。
t_en: As for the new clue, it is probably related to the <color=#dbc291ff>arch</color>. Let's make this our next objective.

t_ch: 总之，这个箭头图案应该就是进一步的线索——至于新的线索，大概是和这个<color=#dbc291ff>「拱门」</color>有关。我们就把它当做下一个目标吧。
t_en: So the arrow pattern must be a clue... and this <color=#dbc291ff>arch</color> is probably the next clue. Let's make this our next objective.

t_ch: 总之，这个箭头图案应该就是进一步的线索——至于新的线索，大概是和这个<color=#dbc291ff>「拱门」</color>有关。我们就把它当做下一个目标吧。
t_en: So the arrow pattern must be a clue... and this <color=#dbc291ff>arch</color> is probably the next clue. Let's make this our next objective.

t_ch: 这里似乎也有敌人在游荡…看来我们不能随心所欲地使唤查宝了。
t_en: There seem to be enemies roaming nearby... We probably shouldn't call Findie right now.

t_ch: 一边小心前进，一边寻找那个可能藏着线索的<color=#dbc291ff>「拱门」</color>吧。
t_en: Let's proceed with caution, and keep an eye out for the <color=#dbc291ff>arch</color> that may contain clues.

t_ch: 关于上次回收处理「特效药」的事，我想再郑重地和你道一次谢。
t_en: I want to thank you again for recovering the wonder medicine. It meant a lot to me.

t_ch: 但光是销毁瓦赫留下的试剂，并不能解决医疗物资短缺的问题。很多患者还是没法及时处理病情，我的药物储备也有些捉襟见肘。
t_en: But disposing of Vache's reagents isn't enough to solve the medical supply shortage. Many of my patients can't be seen promptly, and my stock of medicine is running low.

t_ch: 多亏了你们，通向上层区的「炉心」通道重新开放了。借着这个机会，我想回上层区一趟，争取一些支援。
t_en: Thanks to you, the route to the Overworld through the Furnace Core has reopened. I'd like to use it to return to the surface and see if I can get some help.

t_ch: 怎么个「争取」法呢？
t_en: Help from whom?

t_ch: 我一猜你就会对这件事感兴趣。
t_en: I knew you'd be interested.

t_ch: 需要恢复药？我包里有不少…
t_en: If you need recovery meds, I've got some in my bag...

t_ch: 还是一如既往地幽默呢，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Hehe, funny as always, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 还记得我之前和你说的，有关我的养父母的事吗？
t_en: Remember my adoptive parents I mentioned to you a while ago?

t_ch: 「炉心」通路重新开放之后，我心里就萌生了一个想法。我给养父母的老宅地址写了一封信，拜托桑博替我送达。
t_en: After the reopening of the Furnace Core passage, I had an idea. I wrote a letter and asked Sampo to deliver it to my parents' old address.

t_ch: 我原本没有怀抱太大希望，但没想到桑博居然真的带来了妈妈的回信……
t_en: I didn't have high hopes, but amazingly, Sampo brought me my mother's reply.

t_ch: 原来桑博也是会干人事的啊…
t_en: Sampo being a gentleman... a rare occurrence...

t_ch: 拿到回信的时候，我和你有相同的感受。
t_en: Hehe, that's exactly what I thought when I got her letter.

t_ch: 不会是他伪造的吧？
t_en: Sampo couldn't have forged it, could he?

t_ch: 不会，我们一家人的笔迹我绝对不会认错。别忘了，他们可都是医生。
t_en: No, I recognize my family's handwriting. We are all doctors after all.

t_ch: 爸爸妈妈都还住在老地方，他们虽然已经不再工作在一线，但是和学院、医院的联系应该还在。
t_en: Mom said they still live at the old house, but they're not working on the front lines anymore. They should still have some connections in the medical school and the hospital, though.

t_ch: 如果运气足够好的话，或许我能拜托他们，帮下层区争取到额外的医疗资源。
t_en: If I'm lucky, maybe I can convince them to send extra medical supplies to the Underworld.

t_ch: 当然，除了这件事之外…我想和爸妈团聚的心情也十分迫切。我从妈妈回信的字里行间可以感受到，他们也跟我一样激动。
t_en: Of course, that's not the only reason for me to go back to the surface... I can't wait to visit my parents again, and I can tell from my mom's letter that they feel the same.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，如果可能的话，我希望有你陪我同去。
t_en: If possible, I would like you to go with me, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 当然。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 你的请求，我不会拒绝。
t_en: I won't say no to you.

t_ch: …太好了，这样我就能安下心来了。
t_en: ...Wonderful, that's such a relief.

t_ch: 我已经又给妈妈送去了一封信，约好了会面的地点。我们就在喷泉广场的附近集合吧——别担心，行政区的周边环境我还大抵记得。
t_en: I've sent another letter to my mom telling her to find us near Fountain Plaza. Don't worry, I know my way around the Administrative District.

t_ch: …这里和印象中相比好像没什么变化呢。
t_en: ...this place doesn't seem to have changed much from what I remember.

t_ch: …啊，你来啦。抱歉，刚才看得有些入神了。
t_en: ...Oh, there you are. Sorry, I got distracted watching the crowds.

t_ch: 看看这忙碌的人流，{NICKNAME}——上层区的生活还是一如既往，就好像什么都未曾发生。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, look at the busy flow of people — life in the Overworld carries on as usual, as if nothing has happened.

t_ch: 离我跟妈妈约定的时间还有一阵。我有个想法……
t_en: We're a little early. I've got an idea...

t_ch: 我想给她准备一份礼物。{NICKNAME}，你现在应该比我更熟悉上层区吧？你有什么建议吗？
t_en: I want to buy my mom a gift. {NICKNAME}, you're more of an Overworld local than I am now. Any suggestions?

t_ch: 去长夏花店买一束花吧。
t_en: How about flowers from Eversummer Florist?

t_ch: 花？
t_en: Flowers, huh?

t_ch: 是个好主意呢。在下层呆得太久，我也已经很久没闻到过花朵的芬芳了。
t_en: That's a great idea. I've been underground for so long, I almost forgot what flowers smell like.

t_ch: 那就这么决定吧，{NICKNAME}。可以请你带路吗？
t_en: Flowers it is then, {NICKNAME}. Can you lead the way?

t_ch: 去黄金歌剧院看一场戏吧。
t_en: How about watching a play at Golden Theater?

t_ch: 戏剧…
t_en: A play... hmm...

t_ch: 听上去很有趣，但就怕当天的剧目不合妈妈的胃口。我再想想……
t_en: Sounds fun, but there's a chance it won't suit my mom's taste. Let me think...

t_ch: 对了，我记得上层区有一家花店？不如我们去那边看看吧。
t_en: Oh, yes! I remember there was a florist in the Overworld. How about we head over that way?

t_ch: 在下层待得太久，我也已经很久没闻到过花朵的芬芳了。
t_en: I've been underground for so long, I already forgot what flowers smell like.

t_ch: 去永动机械屋…
t_en: To Neverwinter Workshop...

t_ch: 机械屋？抱歉，我不太明白…还是再想想吧。
t_en: Workshop? I'm not too sure about that... Let's see if we have other options.

t_ch: 对了，我记得上层区有一家花店？不如我们去那边看看吧。
t_en: Oh, yes! I remember there was a florist in the Overworld. How about we head over that way?

t_ch: 在下层待得太久，我也已经很久没闻到过花朵的芬芳了。
t_en: I've been underground for so long, I already forgot what flowers smell like.

t_ch: 「长夏」…这花店一直都叫这名字吗？看来我小时候还没能领会其中的讽刺意味。
t_en: Eversummer... Was that always the name? Seems I was too young to get the irony back then.

t_ch: …抱歉，一不小心就说了些不中听的话。也许这店名只是代表了某种单纯美好的愿望，并没有其他意思。
t_en: ...Sorry, don't mean to sound cynical. Maybe it simply represents the people's hopes up here.

t_ch: 现在想想，对于我们这一家来说，「送花」还真是件有仪式感的事呢。爸爸、妈妈、瓦赫、还有我…只要遇上了值得纪念的事情，我们总会靠送花来表达喜悦。
t_en: Now that I think about it, sending flowers is quite the tradition in my family. Mom, dad, Vache, and I... whenever something good happened, we sent each other flowers to celebrate.

t_ch: 你愿意听我讲讲有关花的故事吗，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Do you want to hear a flower story, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 跟我讲讲吧，娜塔。
t_en: Let's hear one, Nat.

t_ch: 养母喜欢的花是…
t_en: What was your mother's favorite flower...?

t_ch: 每年他们的结婚纪念日，爸爸都会送妈妈一束红纸包扎的「球牡丹」。
t_en: Each year for their anniversary, my dad would send my mom a bouquet of Ball Peonies wrapped in red paper.

t_ch: 听说「球牡丹」的花语是「永不磨灭的爱」——用这几个字来形容他们的婚姻，的确再合适不过了。
t_en: I heard that Ball Peonies represent "love that knows no end..." It does fit their marriage.

t_ch: 养父喜欢的花是…
t_en: What was your father's favorite flower...?

t_ch: 我的养父是贝洛伯格名气响亮的外科医生。也正因如此，他的每台手术都是医界的焦点…他总是背负着巨大的压力。
t_en: My father is a famous surgeon in Belobog. Because of his fame, people in medical circles paid great attention to his surgeries... He always had a lot of stress to deal with.

t_ch: 他每完成一台高难度的手术，我和妈妈都会去买一朵「暖阳花」，别在他医务服的前口袋上。
t_en: Each time he finished a tricky operation, my mom and I would buy a single stem of Sunshine, then pin it on the chest pocket of his apron.

t_ch: 「暖阳花」的花语是「温暖、向往光明」…对于濒临死亡的患者来说，爸爸他应该就是唯一的那束光吧？
t_en: Sunshines represent "warmth and love of light..." For patients on the verge of death, my dad might have been the only light to penetrate the darkness around them.

t_ch: 瓦赫喜欢的花是…
t_en: What was Vache's favorite flower...?

t_ch: 瓦赫…要说我们家谁和花的缘分最浅，应该就属他了吧？
t_en: Vache... Out of everyone in my family, I think he probably appreciated flowers the least.

t_ch: 但我记得很清楚，他拿到医学院录取通知书的那天，爸妈带我去花店买了最贵的一株「侯爵夫人」。
t_en: Still, I remember the day he received his offer from the medical academy. My parents took me to the florist and we bought him the most expensive Marquise stem in the shop.

t_ch: 把那株花送给哥哥的时候，我看到他露出了笑意。你肯定想不到，要让那个人笑一笑是件多难的事。
t_en: When we gave him the flower, he failed to hide his smile. You can't imagine how hard it was to get him to smile.

t_ch: 娜塔喜欢的花是…
t_en: What's your favorite flower...?

t_ch: 我从小一个人长大，完全不知道自己的出生日月。养父母把我带到上层区以后，就把领养我的日子当做我的生日来过。
t_en: As an orphan, I never knew my birthday. My adoptive parents took me to the Overworld and made the day I joined the family my birthday.

t_ch: 每年的这一天，爸妈都会送给我一束「初雪八落」，花的枝数等同于我来到这个家中的年限。
t_en: Each year on that day, my parents would give me a bouquet of First Snow. The number of stems represented how many years had passed since I joined the family.

t_ch: 后来我才知道，「初雪八落」的花语里有「美好的回忆」这层意思。我还没来得及感谢他们…感谢他们给了我美好的童年。
t_en: I only found out later that First Snow represents "beautiful memories." My parents gave me a beautiful childhood, and I still haven't had the chance to thank them for that...

t_ch: 我清楚了，咱们去挑花吧。
t_en: I see. Shall we go to the florist?

t_ch: 抱歉，现在没有那个心情…
t_en: Sorry, not right now...

t_ch: …也对，都是些不值一提的陈年旧事而已，不值得浪费你的时间。
t_en: ...You're right, not the best moment for distant memories, I don't want to waste your time.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，能请你为我挑一束送给母亲的花吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, will you choose some flowers for my mom?

t_ch: 对我来说，这些花各自都有不同的涵义。让我亲自挑选的话，肯定耗上很久都拿不定主意。
t_en: To me, every flower carries a different meaning — it would take me ages to decide.

t_ch: 放心吧，我觉得爸妈不会太介意花束的品种。那个店员姑娘在盯着我们看呢，快去吧。
t_en: Don't worry. I don't think my parents will be picky about the flowers they get. Quick, that florist is looking at us.

t_ch: 怎么了，{NICKNAME}？不去挑花吗？
t_en: Oh, hey {NICKNAME}. I thought you went to choose some flowers?

t_ch: 还是说…你想听听有关我的家人和花的故事？
t_en: Or... did you want to hear some family flower stories?

t_ch: 跟我讲讲吧，娜塔。
t_en: Let's hear one, Nat.

t_ch: 养母喜欢的花是…
t_en: What was your mother's favorite flower...?

t_ch: 每年他们的结婚纪念日，爸爸都会送妈妈一束红纸包扎的「球牡丹」。

t_en: I heard that Ball Peonies represent "love that knows no end..." It does fit their marriage.

t_en: What's your favorite flower...?

t_ch: 我从小一个人长大，完全不知道自己的出生日月。养父母把我带到上层区以后，就把领养我的日子当做我的生日来过。

t_en: Each year on that day, my parents would give me a bouquet of First Snow. The number of stems represented how many years had passed since I joined the family.

t_en: I only found out later that First Snow represents "beautiful memories." My parents gave me a beautiful childhood, and I still haven't had the chance to thank them for that...

t_ch: 我了解了，现在就去挑花。
t_en: I see. I'm going to go pick out some flowers.

t_ch: 没什么，我现在就去挑花。
t_en: Nothing. I'm going to go pick out some flowers.

t_ch: 谢谢你愿意为我做这些，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thank you for doing this, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 又见面了客人，瓦丝卡为您服务！您如果需要一些买花的建议的话，可以随时来问我。
t_en: Nice to see you again! Vaska at your service. If you need some help picking the right flowers, feel free to let me know.

t_ch: 该帮娜塔莎挑什么花呢…
t_en: What flowers should I pick for Nat...

t_ch: 一束「球牡丹」。
t_en: A bouquet of Ball Peonies, please.

t_ch: 我明白了，要一束「球牡丹」对吗？稍等，我马上帮您挑选……
t_en: Aww... Ball Peonies, is it? One second, I'll get it ready for you...

t_ch: 一束「暖阳花」。
t_en: A bouquet of Sunshines, please.

t_ch: 我明白了，要一束「暖阳花」对吗？稍等，我马上帮您挑选……
t_en: Hm? Sunshines, is it? One second, I'll get it ready for you...

t_ch: 一株「侯爵夫人」。
t_en: A bouquet of Marquises, please.

t_ch: 我明白了，要一株「侯爵夫人」对吗？稍等，我马上帮您挑选……
t_en: Aww... Marquises, is it? One second, I'll get it ready for you...

t_ch: 一束「初雪八落」。
t_en: A bouquet of First Snow, please.

t_ch: 我明白了，要一束「初雪八落」对吗？稍等，我马上帮您挑选……
t_en: Aww... First Snow, is it? One second, I'll get it ready for you...

t_ch: …久等了，这是您要的花，请拿好。欢迎下次再来。
t_en: There you go, sorry for the wait. Hope to see you again soon!

t_ch: 等待瓦丝卡为你挑选鲜花……
t_en: You wait for Vaska to get the flowers for you...

t_ch: 妈妈！
t_en: Mom!

t_ch: 娜塔…是娜塔吗？快，快过来，让妈妈好好看看你……
t_en: Nat... Nat! Is that you? Oh come here, my darling...

t_ch: 太久了…实在过去太久了，妈妈……
t_en: It's been too long, mom... too long.

t_ch: …抱歉，奥森先生。你还好吗？谢谢你这十几年来一直陪在爸妈身边。
t_en: Sorry, I don't usually get this emotional... How have you been, Mr. Oswell? Thank you so much for taking care of my parents these last ten years.

t_ch: 我很好，娜塔莎小姐，我很好…您出落成一个大美人了啊！可惜老爷他……
t_en: I'm doing fine, Miss Natasha, just fine... Look at you! You've blossomed into a fine young woman. Such a pity that your father...

t_ch: …爸爸他？对啊，怎么没看到他人呢？他是不是——
t_en: ...Dad? I was wondering where he is. Is he...
Ah...

t_ch: …是三年前，娜塔。不用难过…他走的时候没有痛苦。
t_en: ...It was three years ago, Nat. No need to be sad... he passed away very peacefully.

t_ch: 对不起，妈妈…对不起，这十几年来我没能陪在你们身边。也让你受累了，奥森先生。
t_en: I'm so sorry mom... I'm so sorry. For ten years, I left you both in the Overworld. Mr. Oswell, I'm sorry you had to bear all this weight.

t_ch: 不必这样，小姐…能一直照顾老爷和夫人是我的荣幸。
t_en: Don't be, my dear... It is my honor to look after your parents.

t_ch: 而且上下层的封锁也不是你的错，女儿。你不用为此感到自责。
t_en: It's not your fault that they sealed off the passage between the surface and the underground. Don't be so harsh on yourself, daughter.

t_ch: 妈妈…我真的有很多话想对你说。但眼下还有一件更紧急的事需要你帮忙。
t_en: Mom... I have so much to say to you, but there's an urgent matter that needs your help.

t_ch: 抱歉，我也希望这能是一次温情的重逢…但现在下层区还有很多我关心的人，他们正在遭受病痛的折磨，而你是我眼下唯一能求助的人。
t_en: I apologize. I wish this were a happier reunion... but there are too many suffering with sickness in the Underworld. You're the only person I can go to for help.

t_ch: 你不用道歉，孩子…我听闻了一些你在下层的事。我知道你救了很多人的命。你爸爸他一定会为你骄傲的。
t_en: There's no need to apologize, my child... I've heard of your deeds down there and I know you've saved many lives. Your father... he would be proud of you.

t_ch: 我们家在医学院的人脉很深，不管你需要的是药物或是文献资料，应该都不成问题。
t_en: We have many connections in the medical academy. No matter what you need, be it medicine or data, I should be able to get it for you.

t_ch: 去履行你作为医生的职责吧，娜塔。至于我们家人之间的事，来日方长……
t_en: Be the doctor you always were, Nat. We have all the time in the world to catch up later...

t_ch: 真是位伟大的母亲。
t_en: She's a great mother.

t_ch: 她一直都是这样，从没改变。
t_en: She's always been there for me.

t_ch: 你父亲的事…我很遗憾。
t_en: I'm sorry about your father...

t_ch: 没关系，{NICKNAME}。我其实早有心理准备了…虽然从妈妈口中确认这个消息，我的心头还是会刺痛。
t_en: It's alright, {NICKNAME}. I was prepared... Although it still hurts to hear it from mom.

t_ch: 有了妈妈的帮助，下层区药物短缺的情况肯定能得到缓解。这样一来，很多走投无路的病人应该也能看到希望了。
t_en: With her help, we'll be able to ease the Underworld's medical supply shortage. That's good news for patients who'd lost hope.

t_ch: 我很想花上一整天的时间，坐下来跟她叙叙旧。可惜现在还不是时候。
t_en: I really wish I could spend the whole day talking to her about the old days. But sadly now is not the time.

t_ch: 我们回下层吧，{NICKNAME}。得把这个好消息告诉那些需要帮助的人。
t_en: Let's head back to the Underworld, {NICKNAME}. We need to spread the good news.

t_ch: 娜塔莎和养母紧紧地拥抱在一起……
t_en: Natasha and her adoptive mother share a warm embrace...

t_ch: 商议了为下层区提供药物援助的细节……
t_en: You discuss the details about the medicine supply work in the Underworld...

t_ch: 娜塔莎小姐！请留步！
t_en: Miss Natasha! One moment please!

t_ch: 奥森先生？你不是陪着妈妈先回去了吗？怎么……
t_en: Mr. Oswell? I thought you headed back home with mom. How come...?

t_ch: 娜塔莎小姐，抱歉！其实…其实我有重要的事必须当面告诉您。
t_en: I apologize, Miss Natasha! As a matter of fact... there's something I have to tell you in person.

t_ch: …是不能当着妈妈的面说的事吗？
t_en: Something that mom isn't supposed to know?

t_ch: 您还是和小时候一样，聪明又敏锐。实际上…上下层封锁后的头两年，我收到了很多封瓦赫少爷的来信。
t_en: You haven't changed a bit — smart and sensitive. The fact of the matter is... in the first two years after the passage was closed off, I received many letters from Master Vache.

t_ch: …什么？瓦赫的信？奥森先生，你确定吗？
t_en: ...What? Letters from Vache? Are you certain, Mr. Oswell?

t_ch: 当然，小姐，我不可能认错少爷的笔迹，您看过以后也会明白的。
t_en: Of course, Miss. I recognized his handwriting. You'll see what I mean when you read the letters yourself.

t_ch: 其实…少爷那些信都是写给您的。收件地址填的都是下层区，但因为封锁令，这些信件都被退回到了老宅，到了我手上。
t_en: Actually... those letters were intended for your eyes only. The delivery address was the Underworld, but because of the sealing off of the passageway, the letters were sent back to your old house, to me.

t_ch: 我不明白——你没有把信转交给爸妈吗？
t_en: I don't understand — did you not show them to my parents?

t_ch: 我…对不起，小姐，我出于好奇打开了其中一封。瓦赫少爷在信里嘱咐您，一定不要让老爷和夫人知道他的事……
t_en: I... I'm sorry, Miss. I opened one of them out of curiosity. Master Vache stressed in the letter that your parents must not know what happened to him...

t_ch: 所以我把它们都藏到了安全的地方，等着您回到上层区的那天再予转交。
t_en: That's why I put the letters in a secure place, waiting to hand them to you when we could meet again.

t_ch: 我…娜塔莎小姐，我也不知道自己的选择对不对。我只是不想看到老爷和夫人被思念之苦折磨……
t_en: I... I'm not sure if I did the right thing, Miss Natasha. I just didn't want your parents to feel that their son was so close and yet so far away...

t_ch: …你把信带在身上了吗，奥森先生？给我看看吧。
t_en: ...Do you have the letters on you, Mr. Oswell? Let me take a look.

t_ch: 当然，当然。给——您肯定能认出来，这绝对是少爷的笔迹。
t_en: Of course, of course — here... You can tell it's the young master's handwriting.

t_ch: 「透骨的寒冷让我明白了自己的傲慢。一直在温室中长大的孩子，怎么可能领悟驱散严寒的魔法？」
t_en: "The cold has revealed to me my arrogance. How can a child who grew up in warmth ever begin to comprehend a cure to bitter cold."

t_ch: 他…瓦赫，即使在离开城市之后，他仍然没有放弃对「风雪免疫」的研究。
t_en: He... Vache didn't give up on his research into Blizzard Immunity... even after he left the city.

t_ch: 「我寄宿在被遗弃的屋檐下，在风雪里不断实验、突破，再实验、再突破。我在颤抖，但不是因为寒冷，而是因为我正一步步接近成功……」
t_en: "I live in abandoned houses under broken roofs and continue my experiments. One step after another, I've made breakthroughs. I'm trembling, not because of the cold... but because I can feel I'm getting closer to the answer I seek..."

t_ch: 如果这信里的话属实…那瓦赫一定是在雪原的某处搭建了自己的实验室，甚至还获得了一些成果。但这怎么可能……
t_en: If what's in here is true, then Vache must have built a lab somewhere in the Snow Plains... and even made progress in his research. But how can that be...

t_ch: 你相信他的话吗？
t_en: Do you believe it?

t_ch: 他没必要在信里对我撒谎。当然也有一种可能，或许在写下这些文字的时候，他已经彻底疯了……
t_en: There'd be no point in him lying to me in the letters. Still, it's possible he may have gone completely insane before he started writing them...

t_ch: 也许他已经疯了…
t_en: Maybe he went mad...

t_ch: 或许吧，但如果他写下这些字的时候还清醒着…
t_en: It's possible. But what if he was still sane when he wrote this down...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我想出一次城，去寻找哥哥留下的研究成果。你愿意和我同去吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I need to leave the city to recover my brother's research data. Are you willing to come with me?

t_ch: 当然。
t_en: Sure thing.

t_ch: 出城？娜塔莎小姐，这未免太冒险了……
t_en: Leave the city? You are taking too much risk, Miss Natasha...

t_ch: 城墙外很危险…
t_en: It's dangerous outside the city walls...

t_ch: 这位{F#小姐}{M#先生}说得对，贝洛伯格的城墙外遍地都是危险……
t_en: This {F#young lady}{M#young gentleman} is right. Danger is everywhere beyond the walls of Belobog...

t_ch: 我知道，奥森先生。但我身边有这位拯救了下层区的英雄，{F#她}{M#他}会保护好我的，对吧？
t_en: I know, Mr. Oswell. But I have a hero with me — {F#she}{M#he} saved the Underworld. I'm sure {F#she'll}{M#he'll} protect me, right?

t_ch: 瓦赫…这么多年过去了，你该不会还在那片风雪里……
t_en: Vache... It's been so many years. Are you still alone in the blizzard...?

t_ch: …没什么，只是我的自言自语而已。走吧，{NICKNAME}，该去追逐往日的影子了。
t_en: ...Ignore me... I'm just talking to myself. Let's go, {NICKNAME}. It's time to face my past head-on.

t_ch: 如此严寒…实在难以想象有人能在这种环境下生存。
t_en: Such harsh cold... It's hard to imagine anyone surviving in these conditions.

t_ch: 除非真的有「风雪免疫」这种药物的存在……
t_en: Unless Blizzard Immunity is real...

t_ch: 我们快些走吧，{NICKNAME}。得在身体冻僵之前找到瓦赫留下的线索。
t_en: Let's hurry up, {NICKNAME}. We need to find any clues that Vache may have left behind before both of us become ice sculptures.

t_ch: 你看，这里有些类似实验仪器的东西，说不定是瓦赫留下来的。
t_en: Here, this looks like lab equipment. Maybe Vache left it behind.

t_ch: 但也只是些已经废弃的试管、烧杯，还有其它容器。没有留下任何试剂样品，甚至没有用来记录实验结果的笔记……
t_en: Still, it's all just used test tubes, beakers, and other vessels. No sign of any sample reagents, or lab notes for that matter...

t_ch: 也许是因为条件太艰苦了…
t_en: Maybe things were too harsh here for record-keeping...

t_ch: 我了解哥哥，他对医学实验的态度严谨得可怕。不管条件有多艰辛，他也绝对不会漏记任何一次实验结果。
t_en: I know my brother he's — meticulous when it comes to medical experiments. There's no way he wouldn't document his results.

t_ch: 说不定是因为他的记性很好。
t_en: Maybe his memory's too good for lab notes.

t_ch: 我了解哥哥，他对医学实验的态度严谨得可怕。他绝对不会漏记任何一次实验结果。
t_en: I know my brother he's — meticulous when it comes to medical experiments. There's no way he wouldn't document his results.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我想再向雪原深处探索一阵。你还能坚持住吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I want to go deeper into the Snow Plains. Can you press on any further?

t_ch: 呵…温度越来越低了呢。但我可不想就这样半途而废。
t_en: *shivering* Gosh... The temperature keeps getting lower... but we can't give up halfway.

t_ch: 「开拓」在保护我，反倒是你…
t_en: Trailblaze protects me. It's you I'm worried about...

t_ch: 不用担心我，可别小看了「地火」领袖的体质。
t_en: Hehe, no need. Don't you underestimate the leader of Wildfire.

t_ch: 我可以把体温借给你。
t_en: I can warm you up if you'd like.

t_ch: 真贴心，好意我先心领了——但你可别小看了「地火」领袖的体质啊。
t_en: Hehe, that's very kind of you. Don't you underestimate the leader of Wildfire.

t_ch: 我们继续前进吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Let's press on then, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 虽然我从没这么说过，但这次…希望那些筑城者信奉的神祇能分给我们些运气。
t_en: This may not sound like me, but... I hope that deity the Architects worship can grant us some luck.

t_ch: …市民？在城墙外面？喂，你们两个，给我站住！
t_en: ...Citizens? Outside the walls? Hey, you two, stop right there!

t_ch: 那个铁卫，他是在冲着我们大喊大叫吗？
t_en: That's a Silvermane Guard. Is he yelling at us?

t_ch: 啊，我差点忘了…擅自离开贝洛伯格是违法行为——看来我们有得解释了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Ah, I'd almost forgotten... No one can leave Belobog without authorization. Looks like we need to talk our way out of this, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 虽然你们的态度很诚恳…但想让我相信这些话，未免也……
t_en: I can't say you sound suspicious, but... I can't just take your word for it either...

t_ch: 队长，你看这位医生的项链…或许她说的是真的。
t_en: Captain, look at her necklace... Maybe she's telling the truth.

t_ch: 那是…和瓦赫医生一样的……
t_en: It's... the same as Dr. Vache's.

t_ch: 我和哥哥确实经常戴一样的项链…怎么了？莫非你们认识瓦赫医生？
t_en: My brother does wear an identical necklace... Do you know him?

t_ch: 您真的是那位医生的妹妹吗？瓦赫医生，他可是我们不少弟兄的救命恩人啊。
t_en: Are you really his sister? Dr. Vache saved a lot of my soldiers!

t_ch: 救命…恩人？
t_en: Saved... your soldiers?

t_ch: 是啊。你看我们这些在城外巡逻的铁卫，哪个不是冒着生命危险在执勤？
t_en: Yes. Take a look at the guards patrolling outside the city. All of them are risking their lives.

t_ch: 先不提四处游荡的怪物，光是因为这极寒天气殉职的弟兄，每年就不止几个。
t_en: Aside from the monster-related casualties, we lose men to frostbite every year.

t_ch: 多亏了那位医生留下的抗寒药，我们才能安心地在这雪原里扎营、巡视，不用担心自己会被冻成冰雕。
t_en: But thanks to the medicine the doctor leaves us, my soldiers are able to withstand the cold. They can make camps in the Snow Plains as footholds for our patrols... If it weren't for the doctor, we would have long since frozen to death.

t_ch: 抗寒药…「风雪免疫」……
t_en: Medicine... Blizzard Immunity...

t_ch: 瓦赫现在在哪？
t_en: Where is Vache now?

t_ch: 这…我们也不清楚。
t_en: ...We don't know.

t_ch: 瓦赫医生总是神出鬼没的，我们小队只撞见过他一次。他好像…不太爱说话，每次都只是把药箱留在特定的位置，等巡逻队自行来取。
t_en: Doctor Vache is... elusive. My squad only met him once, and he didn't seem like the talkative type. He leaves the medicine at specific locations and our patrols go there to collect it.

t_ch: 他给的药功效极强，只要一小瓶就能让人连续一个星期感受不到寒冷。
t_en: The medicine is extremely potent — a small vial can keep someone warm for a week.

t_ch: 他留下的药够你们所有人分吗？
t_en: Is there enough for all of you?

t_ch: 这药的功效极强，只要一小瓶就能让人连续一个星期感受不到寒冷。之前留下的存货，到现在还剩下一大半。
t_en: The medicine is extremely potent — a small vial can keep someone warm for a week. We have over half our stock left.

t_ch: 瓦赫医生总是神出鬼没的，我们小队只撞见过他一次。他好像…不太爱说话，每次都只是把药箱留在特定的位置，等巡逻队自行来取。
t_en: Doctor Vache is... elusive. My squad only met him once, and he didn't seem like the talkative type. He leaves the medicine at specific locations and our patrols go there to collect it.

t_ch: 不过话说回来，距离上次有人撞见瓦赫医生已经过去很长时间了…大概有两三年了吧？希望他还平安……
t_en: Speaking of the doctor, it's been a long time since we last saw him... at least two or three years. I hope he's alive and well...

t_ch: 所以，他真的在雪原里取得了研究突破……
t_en: So he really did have a breakthrough in the Snow Plains...

t_ch: 那你们呢？这药物有没有什么副作用？
t_en: Did the medicine have any side effects for you?

t_ch: …副作用？完全没感觉到。要说真有什么不方便的地方，那就是在药劲没过去之前回不了城……
t_en: ...Side effects? Not that I know of. If there were a problem, it'd be that we couldn't go back to the city until the effect had worn off...

t_ch: ——因为全身上下都在发热，一回城里就会闷得满身大汗，哈哈哈。
t_en: ...because the city would be too hot for us. We'd break a sweat just sitting idle, haha.

t_ch: 谢谢你们提供的信息。我们打算再深入雪原一些，寻找我哥哥的下落。
t_en: Thanks for the information. We're planning to head deeper into the Snow Plains to find my brother.

t_ch: …看来你们是已经下定决心了。我很想派人护送你们，但出外勤的小队绝对不能擅离职守……
t_en: ...Seems like you've made up your mind. I'd really like to send some of my men with you, but we can't leave our posts.

t_ch: 这样吧，这一瓶瓦赫医生的抗寒药，请两位拿着。如果你们叙述的经历都是真的，我觉得两位肯定有能力保护自己。
t_en: Here, take this medicine. If what you two told me is true, I'm sure you're more than capable of handling yourselves out there.

t_ch: 谢谢你，队长。那我们就收下了。
t_en: Thank you, captain. We'll take it with us.

t_ch: 向巡逻的银鬃铁卫解释了你们的身份和来意……
t_en: You explain your identity and purpose to the patrolling Silvermane Guard.

t_ch: 等一下！那边有个巨大的家伙。
t_en: Hold on! That's a big guy over there.

t_ch: 你也看到了吗？那怪物身后好像有什么东西。
t_en: Did you see that? There's something on the monster's back.

t_ch: 我准备好战斗了，{NICKNAME}。安心把你的背后交给医生吧。
t_en: I'm ready, {NICKNAME}. The doctor's got your back.

t_ch: 对了，「风雪免疫」...要不要喝下它，就由你来决定吧。
t_en: Oh, the Blizzard Immunity. Shall we drink it? It's your call.

t_ch: 服下瓦赫的「风雪免疫」。
t_en: Let's drink the Blizzard Immunity.

t_ch: 不服用药物，直接与敌人战斗。
t_en: Let's not drink it, it's time to fight some enemies.

t_ch: 你感觉到了吗，{NICKNAME}？瓦赫的药物似乎起效果了。
t_en: Can you feel it, {NICKNAME}? It seems like Vache's medicine is working.

t_ch: 如此温暖……
t_en: So warm...

t_ch: 结束了呢，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It's done, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 离得这么近观察你战斗的样子，我还是头一次。帅气又利落，不愧是下层区的救星。
t_en: That was my first time seeing you in battle... you've got some moves, don't you? No wonder you were able to save the Underworld.

t_ch: 我们去查看一下那边的情况吧。我有预感，应该能找到和瓦赫有关的东西……
t_en: Let's have a look over there. My gut tells me we should find something connected to Vache...

t_ch: 这是，实验记录……
t_en: These are... records of his experiments.

t_ch: 是瓦赫的笔迹，这里一定就是他最后的实验据点。{NICKNAME}，你也过来看看吧。
t_en: It's Vache's handwriting. This must be the location of his final lab. {NICKNAME}, come have a look.

t_ch: 与娜塔莎一起，为瓦赫在雪中立了一座墓碑……
t_en: You and Natasha put up a headstone for Vache...

t_ch: 他最后一次实验的时间是接近两年之前。在那之后发生了什么？看实验笔记上的内容，他应该还在寻求改良「风雪免疫」的方法。
t_en: His last experiment was nearly two years ago. What happened after that? His notes seem to suggest he was working to improve Blizzard Immunity.

t_ch: 哥哥…你到底去了哪里？你到底……
t_en: Brother... where are you? What happened...?

t_ch: 娜塔，就到这儿吧。
t_en: Let's call it a day, Nat.

t_ch: 我们该回去了…
t_en: It's time we headed back...

t_ch: 抱歉，我刚才没控制好情绪。
t_en: Sorry, I needed to take a moment.

t_ch: 从看到奥森先生保存的那封信开始，我就一直怀有一丝侥幸心理。逼着你陪我来到这么凶险的地方，是我太过任性了。
t_en: After seeing the letters Mr. Oswell kept for me, I was hoping things might've ended differently. I shouldn't have asked you to come with me to such a dangerous place.

t_ch: 你很想念他，对吗？
t_en: You miss him, right?
...I do.

t_ch: 瓦赫对你来说，究竟是…
t_en: What is your feeling towards Vache...

t_ch: …我的家人，也是我曾经追逐的目标。
t_en: ...He was my family, and someone I used to look up to.

t_ch: 我无法原谅他的行为。但我也没法控制自己对兄长的思念……
t_en: I can't forgive him for what he's done. But nor can I say I don't miss him...

t_ch: 我…也许我该感到高兴才对。他的坚持最终还是获得了回报，哪怕没能做到完美的地步…也许他能获得一丝安宁……
t_en: I... maybe I should feel glad. The medicine might not have been what he wanted it to be, but it came to something... Maybe he found some inner peace...

t_ch: 被他伤害的人们不会感到安宁。
t_en: Too bad his victims won't ever share that peace.

t_ch: …你说得没错。瓦赫犯下的恶行无法宽恕，他也愧对「医生」这个称呼。
t_en: ...You're right. There can be no forgiveness for the crimes he committed. He didn't deserve the title of "doctor."

t_ch: 人们会记得瓦赫医生。
t_en: People will remember Dr. Vache.

t_ch: …记得他曾是伤害无辜的加害者，记得他曾经犯下的恶行。瓦赫愧对「医生」这个称呼，这点我再清楚不过。
t_en: ...They'll remember him as a murderer... as someone whose evil experiments brought great harm to the innocent. Vache doesn't deserve to be remembered as a "doctor." I know that very well.

t_ch: 但哪怕出自「恶魔」之手，这些「风雪免疫」还是可以造福后人。
t_en: But maybe this Blizzard Immunity can now bring benefit to others... even if it was made by a monster.

t_ch: 还真是矛盾啊，人这种生物……
t_en: Human beings really are conflicted life forms...

t_ch: 接下来你打算怎么办？
t_en: What now?

t_ch: 我要带走这些实验记录和资料，继续瓦赫未竟的研究。总有一天，最完美的「风雪免疫」将会问世。
t_en: I'll take his records and data with me and continue what he started. One day, the world will see a perfect Blizzard Immunity.

t_ch: 但在离开这里之前，{NICKNAME}——我还想请你帮我一个忙。我向你保证，这是最后一次……
t_en: Before we leave, {NICKNAME}, I'd like to ask for another favor — the last one, I promise...

t_ch: …这样就可以了。希望它能在风雪里多伫立一段时间。
t_en: ...That should do. Hope it can withstand the snow.

t_ch: 墓碑上应该有墓志铭才对。
t_en: You ought to put an epitaph on the headstone.

t_ch: 好像是这样没错。让我思考一下……
t_en: You're right. Let me think...

t_ch: 瓦赫值得你为他做这么多吗？
t_en: Is Vache worth you doing all this for him?

t_ch: 我不知道。这只是我脑海里一闪而过的想法而已。
t_en: I don't know. It's just something that suddenly occurred to me.

t_ch: 话说回来，墓碑上一般都会有题字，对吧？让我想想……
t_en: People write things on headstones, right? Let me think...

t_ch: …「瓦赫——吾兄」。
t_en: "...Here lies Vache, my brother."

t_ch: 这样就可以了，{NICKNAME}。谢谢你帮我了断这最后一桩心事。
t_en: That'll do. Thank you for helping me, {NICKNAME}. I feel like a great weight has been lifted.

t_ch: 再见，哥哥。希望你还记得爸爸的教诲。
t_en: So long, brother. Hope you don't forget dad's teaching.

t_ch: 「医生没有选择患者的权力，孩子们——记住，永远不要放弃任何一个向你求救的人。永远不要。」
t_en: "A doctor does not get to choose his or her patient, my children. Remember, never say no to those who come to you for help. Never."

t_ch: 挑好了吗？让我看看你选了什么花吧。
t_en: All done? May I have a look?

t_ch: 球牡丹……
t_en: Ball Peonies...

t_ch: 很好的选择呢，{NICKNAME}。不知道爸爸他还会不会在结婚纪念日的时候给妈妈送花……
t_en: Nice pick, {NICKNAME}. I wonder if dad still sends mom flowers on their anniversary...

t_ch: 我就知道你的选择一定能让我满意。爸妈收到这花，一定会喜出望外的。
t_en: I knew you'd pick the right flowers. Mom and dad will be so happy.

t_ch: 走吧，约好的时间快到了。可不能让老人家等太久。
t_en: Let's go, my mom will be there soon. Best not to leave old folks waiting!

t_ch: 挑好了吗？让我看看你选了什么花吧。
t_en: All done? May I have a look?

t_ch: 暖阳花……
t_en: Sunshines...

t_ch: 很好的选择呢，{NICKNAME}。爸爸他年纪已经很大了，应该不用再亲自操刀手术了吧？希望他身体还好……
t_en: Nice pick, {NICKNAME}. Dad's not young anymore, he probably doesn't have to do operations solo these days. I hope he's looking after himself...

t_ch: 我就知道你的选择一定能让我满意。爸妈收到这花，一定会喜出望外的。
t_en: I knew you'd pick the right flowers. Mom and dad will be so happy.

t_ch: 走吧，约好的时间快到了。可不能让老人家等太久。
t_en: Let's go, my mom will be there soon. Best not to leave old folks waiting!

t_ch: 挑好了吗？让我看看你选了什么花吧。
t_en: All done? May I have a look?

t_ch: 侯爵夫人…这么名贵的花……
t_en: Marquises... Expensive...

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。不瞒你说，看到这朵花的一瞬间，我立刻就联想到了瓦赫…啊，不用担心，我想到的是一些幸福的回忆。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. To be honest with you, it reminds me of Vache... Don't worry, good memories only.

t_ch: 我就知道你的选择一定能让我满意。爸妈收到这花，一定会喜出望外的。
t_en: I knew you'd pick the right flowers. Mom and dad will be so happy.

t_ch: 走吧，约好的时间快到了。可不能让老人家等太久。
t_en: Let's go, my mom will be there soon. Best not to leave old folks waiting!

t_ch: 挑好了吗？让我看看你选了什么花吧。
t_en: All done? May I have a look?

t_ch: 初雪八落……
t_en: First Snow...

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。这束花让我想起了童年…真是一段无忧无虑的时光。就和它们花瓣的颜色一样，洁白、纯真。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. It reminds me of carefree childhood days... Those times were pure and innocent, just like these petals.

t_ch: 我就知道你的选择一定能让我满意。爸妈收到这花，一定会喜出望外的。
t_en: I knew you'd pick the right flowers. Mom and dad will be so happy.

t_ch: 走吧，约好的时间快到了。可不能让老人家等太久。
t_en: Let's go, my mom will be there soon. Best not to leave old folks waiting!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，有时间吗？我又有一件事想请你帮忙。一件…比较私人的事。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, can I talk to you for a moment? I have a favor to ask. Something... rather personal.

t_ch: 当然有，娜塔。
t_en: Of course, Nat.

t_ch: 有些别的事要忙…
t_en: I have other things to attend to...

t_ch: 没关系，我理解。你肯定在忙很重要的事吧？那就快去吧。
t_en: It's okay, I understand. You must have some important things on your plate? Don't let them get ahead of you.

t_ch: 娜塔，你有事找我吗？
t_en: Nat, do you have something to ask?

t_ch: 就像是静候着好心人将椅子放回原位，珍贵的宝物迫不及待地出现在你的眼前。
t_en: The precious treasure suddenly appears before your eyes, as if it was waiting for a well-meaning person to put the chair back in place.

t_ch: 就像是默默注视着自作主张的举动，灯具的坐台悄悄地移开了位置，珍贵的宝物猝不及防地出现在你的眼前。
t_en: The base of the lamp quietly moved aside to suddenly reveal the precious treasure before your eyes, as if it was silently gazing at your actions.

t_ch: 就像是为了奖励好奇的孩子，原本平坦的地面悄悄地凸出一块，珍贵的宝物猛然出现在你的眼前。
t_en: The flat ground slowly bulges out before you and the precious treasure suddenly appears before your eyes, as if it was rewarding a curious child.

t_ch: 就像是为了鼓励勤勉的士兵，看似随意摆放的沙包发出机关的声音，珍贵的宝物突然出现在你的眼前。
t_en: The sandbags seemingly placed at random give off a mechanical sound and the precious treasure suddenly appears before your eyes, as if it was encouraging diligent troops.

t_ch: （枪架上摆放着「3把枪支」…这和故事书里的描述一致，应该可以放着不管……）
t_en: (Three guns are placed up on the gun rack... This is just like in the book, so I'd better leave it alone...)

t_ch: （士兵会检查4处枪架，确保它们各自放着3把枪支…先去枪架前看看吧。）
t_en: (The soldiers will check the four gun racks to make sure each has three guns... Go to the gun racks and have a look.)

t_ch: （只要确保枪架上摆放着「3把枪支」，那么适当地调整一下枪支的摆放也是可以的吧？）
t_en: (As long as you make sure there are three guns on each rack, switching around the order of the guns won't be a problem — right?)

t_ch: 是你啊，{NICKNAME}！还能在行政区碰见你真是太好了。最近怎么样？
t_en: Hey, it's you, {NICKNAME}! I didn't expect to see you here in the Administrative District. How have you been lately?

t_ch: 好得很，你呢？
t_en: Pretty good. How about you?

t_ch: 那就好！星核的影响消散之后，整个贝洛伯格的氛围明显欢脱了不少，这跟你们的努力脱不了干系。
t_en: Great to hear! Since the Stellaron crisis subsided, Belobog's been feeling a little more cheerful. We're really grateful for your assistance...

t_ch: ——回答你的问题，我最近也过得不错，谢谢关心。
t_en: ...and yes, things are going well for me too — thanks for asking!

t_ch: 不怎么顺，但还过得去。
t_en: Things could be better.

t_ch: 唔…相信我，都会好起来的。
t_en: Oh... Don't worry, things will get better — I'm sure of it.

t_ch: 星核的影响消散之后，整个贝洛伯格的氛围明显欢脱了不少，这跟你们的努力脱不了干系。
t_en: Speaking of things getting better, Belobog's been feeling a little more cheerful since the Stellaron crisis subsided. We couldn't have done it without you.

t_ch: 你之前在贝洛伯格待了那么久，应该路过这里不少次吧？
t_en: You spent so much time in Belobog on your last visit — you must have passed by here before, right?

t_ch: 我身后这幢建筑是贝洛伯格的历史文化博物馆，从它建成至今已经过去六百多个年头了。
t_en: This is Belobog's History and Culture Museum — built more than six hundred years ago.

t_ch: 你们第一次来到这里的时候，正巧赶上十年一度的翻修工程，博物馆没有对访客开放。
t_en: Last time you visited, it was closed for its ten-year renovation.

t_ch: 星核危机过后，我们得以投入更多人力、加速翻修工作，博物馆应该很快就能再次面向公众开放了。
t_en: Now that the Stellaron crisis is over, we've been able to allocate more manpower to the renovation works. The museum should be open to the public again soon.

t_ch: 只是……
t_en: It's just...

t_ch: 只是？
t_en: Just what?

t_ch: 遇上困难了？
t_en: ...You ran into trouble?

t_ch: …翻修工程遇到了一些阻力，银鬃铁卫正在尽力解决。
t_en: ...We encountered a problem during the renovation process. The Silvermane Guards are doing all they can to figure it out.

t_ch: 那个…{NICKNAME}，这里太空旷了，我怕说话漏风。要不我带你到博物馆里面转一圈，顺便聊聊这件事？
t_en: I... *sigh* Sorry, {NICKNAME}, I shouldn't talk about it in the open like this. Hey – why don't I give you a tour of the museum? We can talk inside while I show you around...

t_ch: 当然，前提是你现在有空。
t_en: ...if you have time, that is.

t_ch: 好好聊聊吧。
t_en: That sounds nice.

t_ch: 那我们走吧，进去再聊。
t_en: Great! What are we waiting for?

t_ch: 欢迎光临，{NICKNAME}。这里就是贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, welcome to Belobog's History and Culture Museum.

t_ch: 如你所见，经过为期近百天的翻修，整个博物馆室内现在已经焕然一新。
t_en: As you can see, after nearly <unbreak>100</unbreak> days of renovation, the interior looks brand-new.

t_ch: 不过，在解决一个关键问题之前…博物馆还不能对外开放。
t_en: However... there's a problem that needs to be resolved before it can open to the public.

t_ch: 这座博物馆建立于距今约六百年前，最初修建它的目的是为了纪念初任大守护者阿丽萨•兰德、以及那些在对抗天外入侵者的战役中奉献出生命的先烈。
t_en: The museum was established nearly six hundred years ago as a memorial to honor Alisa Rand, the first supreme guardian, and the martyrs who gave their lives in the battle against the invaders from beyond the sky.

t_ch: 后来，随着时间的推移，纪念馆的展出范畴也逐渐拓宽，直到变成了今天的模样。
t_en: As time went on, new exhibits were continually added to the memorial until it became the museum we see today.

t_ch: 穿过接待厅就到了「综合馆」，也是历史文化博物馆最大的展区。
t_en: Past the Reception Hall, you'll find the General Hall — the museum's largest exhibition room.

t_ch: 这里陈列的展品多种多样，从名家的画作到地髓标本，足以让访客在短短几分钟内掌握贝洛伯格文化的精髓。
t_en: There are all sorts of exhibits on display – paintings by famous artists, samples of the planet's crust... Visitors can experience the essence of Belobog's culture in just a few minutes' time.

t_ch: 如果可能的话，我很希望能马上开放综合馆——城里，尤其是下层区的孩子们，急需一个能接受文化熏陶的环境。
t_en: I really hope the museum can open up soon... The children here need a culturally-enriching environment — especially those from the Underworld...

t_ch: 不过，如我之前所说…我们遇到了一些阻力，急需有心人士的支援。
t_en: ...but like I mentioned earlier, we're in a bit of a bind — we could really use a helping hand.

t_ch: 你看，这就是我提到的「麻烦」。
t_en: Here – this is the issue we're trying to deal with.

t_ch: 展品丢了？
t_en: The exhibit disappeared?

t_ch: 确切地说，是展品失窃了。
t_en: It was stolen.

t_ch: 有小偷？
t_en: There's a thief on the prowl?

t_ch: …哇，你的直觉好敏锐！没错，这件展品是最近才失窃的。
t_en: ...Wow, you're quick! Yes, this exhibit was stolen just recently.

t_ch: 打不开了？看我的——
t_en: So you need help opening this cabinet...

t_ch: 停，停下！你想干嘛——
t_en: Wait! What are you doing!?

t_ch: 唉…别吓我呀。告诉你吧，其实是原本陈列在这个柜子里的展品最近失窃了。
t_en: *sigh* You're in a museum, remember!? Here, let me explain – this exhibit was recently stolen.

t_ch: 这里原本陈列的是<color=#dbc291ff>「流星碎片」</color>，它是综合馆的展品里历史最悠久、也可以说是最具非凡意义的一件。
t_en: It was one of the oldest and most precious items in the General Hall collection — the <color=#dbc291ff>Meteor Fragment</color>.

t_ch: 据说七百年前，天外的军团伴随着流星雨一同降落到了雅利洛-Ⅵ表面，那块碎片就是入侵者曾经荼毒这片大地的铁证……
t_en: It is said that seven hundred years ago, the invaders from beyond the sky descended upon the surface of Jarilo-VI with a meteor shower. The Meteor Fragment is solid evidence that those invaders once tainted our land...

t_ch: …咳，抱歉，老毛病犯了，我不是来给你上历史课的。不夸张地说，如果找不回「流星碎片」，综合馆开放的意义也就失去了一半。
t_en: ...Ahem, sorry, the tour guide in me gets a little carried away — I'm not here to give you a history lesson. Still, it's no exaggeration to say that if the Meteor Fragment isn't recovered, the museum will lose half of its significance.

t_ch: 知道小偷是谁吗？
t_en: Do you know who the thief is?

t_ch: …还不知道，这也是让铁卫头疼的主要原因。
t_en: ...Not yet. That's why this case is such a conundrum for the Silvermane Guards.

t_ch: 造个模型不好吗？
t_en: Can't we just use a replica?

t_ch: …那怎么行！身为博物馆项目的负责人，我可不能容忍这种蒙骗大众的行为……
t_en: ...Out of the question! As the curator of the exhibits here, I would never resort to deceiving the public like that...

t_ch: 当务之急是找到窃贼，拿回失窃的展品。不过我们还没能揪出对方的身份…这也是让铁卫头疼的主要原因。
t_en: Our first priority is finding the thief and recovering the stolen item, but we don't even have a suspect yet... That's why this case is such a conundrum for the Silvermane Guards.

t_ch: 老实说，「流星碎片」不是唯一失窃的展品…但现在的当务之急是先让综合馆对公众开放，后续的追查工作可以暂缓。
t_en: Truth be told, the Meteor Fragment isn't the only item that was stolen... but we want the museum to open as soon as possible. We can worry about the other missing items later.

t_ch: 掌握什么线索了吗？
t_en: Any leads?

t_ch: 当然，我既然想请你帮忙，那自然不能让你从头查起，那未免太耗时耗力了。
t_en: Of course — I wouldn't ask you to start a whole investigation from scratch.

t_ch: 我大概想到是谁干的了。
t_en: I think I know who the culprit is.

t_ch: 啊，你想说桑博？不，我觉得不会。这阵子，我们的人盯他盯得可紧了——听报告说，那家伙最近安分得像个三好市民，一点犯事的苗头都没冒出来。
t_en: Ah, you must be thinking of Sampo, right? I don't think so, our people have been keeping a close eye on him these days. The reports say he's been on his best behavior – not the slightest hint of trouble-making.

t_ch: 据驻扎在下层区的铁卫说，他们最近经常在深夜目击到一些行迹可疑的人物在<color=#dbc291ff>大矿区的隐匿处</color>接头。
t_en: The guards stationed in the Underworld reported that some suspicious characters have been meeting at <color=#dbc291ff>a hideout in the Great Mine</color> late at night.

t_ch: 碍于铁卫刚刚入驻下层不久，而且缺少直接的证据，我们还没能逮捕任何嫌疑人。
t_en: Since the Silvermane Guards are still getting situated in the Underworld and we don't have any direct evidence, we haven't been able to apprehend any suspects.

t_ch: 但如果换成是你的话…我觉得咱们单靠四处打听就能收集到不少有用的信息。
t_en: But if you were to go there yourself and ask around... I think we could gather a lot of useful intel.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你愿意帮我们这个忙吗？
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, would you like to help solve this case?

t_ch: 没问题，我们这就出发吧。
t_en: I'd love to.

t_ch: 你答应了？太好了！这么干脆，不愧是你。那好，我们这就出发去下层区。
t_en: Excellent! Just as I expected — short, sweet, and decisive. To the Underworld it is, then.

t_ch: 稍晚点，我们大矿区见吧。
t_en: Let's meet at the Great Mine and go from there...

t_ch: 噢，这么说，你答应了？太好了！行，那你先去忙自己的，我们下层区见吧。
t_en: ...Sound promising! I'll meet you in the Underworld, then.

t_ch: 这里的条件…真简陋。不知道这些人在这里住了多久了……
t_en: The conditions here are so... rudimentary. I wonder how long those people have been living here...

t_ch: 啊，言归正传。我们联络上了一个「地火」的探子，听说她掌握了些一手的情报。
t_en: ...Back to the investigation. We contacted an agent from Wildfire — apparently she has some first-hand intel.

t_ch: 名字好像是叫…斯薇塔，对，想起来了。走吧，我们过去找她。
t_en: I think her name was... Swetta? Yes, that's right. Let's go pay her a visit.

t_ch: …呀，吓我一跳！
t_en: Ah! Wh—

t_ch: 是你们啊，咻…我还以为是我的行踪暴露了呢。说话小声点，这地方可能到处都是眼线……
t_en: Oh, it's you... Phew, I thought I'd been discovered. Let's try not to make a commotion — you never know who's listening...

t_ch: 已经这么严重了吗？到底有多少人参与了文物偷盗……
t_en: That bad, huh? How many exhibit thieves are there...?

t_ch: 很多——怕你不知道，小姑娘，凡是能赚钱的小道消息，在下层区的传播速度都特别快。
t_en: A lot... I don't know if you're aware, miss, but any intel that makes money spreads like wildfire in the Underworld.

t_ch: 虽说上下层区已经开放有段时间了，但毕竟不是每个人都能第一时间争取到好的机会。大部分人还是得留在下层，想办法养家糊口。
t_en: Travel between the Overworld and the Underworld has been open for some time, but not everyone down here can take advantage of that opportunity. The majority of underworlders have to stay here to feed their families.

t_ch: 据我了解，这文物交易的买卖，里面能榨出的油水可是多得很…啧，要不是我还在给「地火」办事，说不定也会心动。
t_en: From what I hear, smuggling museum artifacts is a lucrative enterprise... Tsk, if I wasn't working for Wildfire, I'd be tempted to get in on the cash myself.

t_ch: 知道偷窃行动是谁组织的了吗？
t_en: Do you know who might be behind the museum thefts?

t_ch: 还不清楚，不过我听说了一些传闻，说不定你们会感兴趣。
t_en: Not for certain, but I've heard some rumors that might be of interest to you.

t_ch: 能赚多少？
t_en: How much can you make?

t_ch: 你不会在动什么歪脑筋吧……
t_en: You aren't getting any funny ideas, are you?

t_ch: 咳，那我说正事了。「地火」也还没查清楚偷盗团伙的头目是谁，但是我听说了一些有趣的传闻，说不定对你们有帮助。
t_en: *sigh* I'll cut to the chase. Wildfire hasn't identified the ringleader yet, but I've heard some interesting rumors that might be helpful to you.

t_ch: 说来听听。
t_en: I'm all ears.

t_ch: 第一件事…我听说这个团伙办事非常张扬，甚至会把招募告示贴的满街都是。你们要是能找到他们的告示，说不定能从印在上面的信息里获取到些线索。
t_en: Firstly, the group in question has a very public presence — they post recruitment notices all over the street. If you can find a notice, you might be able to glean some clues from it.

t_ch: 第二件事，我听说他们干活的风格非常粗犷，一点都不仔细，甚至有人看到他们搬着好几个大箱子临街交易，完全不把周围的矿工当外人。
t_en: Secondly, they're rash and careless in their work. They've been seen carrying large crates of illicit goods around the mines and selling them out in the open, without even trying to hide anything from the miners.

t_ch: 第三件事…我听说矿区有些小孩子也和这事有关联。这点我还没机会查证，你们不妨留意一下。
t_en: Thirdly, I heard there are children in the mines that might be involved. I haven't had a chance to look into it, but keep an eye out.

t_ch: 嗯…我明白了。谢谢你，斯薇塔小姐，你提供的信息肯定能派上用场。
t_en: Hmm... I see. Thanks for the intel, I'm sure it'll help us crack the case.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我想在聚居地再和其他人聊聊，再多收集些线索。
t_en: Well {NICKNAME}, I'm going to head for the settlement and see if I can get more intel from the locals.

t_ch: 可以的话，能拜托你去外面调查一圈吗？等你回来，我们再把碎片拼在一起——麻烦你了。
t_en: In the meantime, do you think you could head out and look for more clues? We can compare notes when you're done – I'm counting on you!

t_ch: 有三件事值得留意……
t_en: Remember the three things I told you...

t_ch: 咦？你们怎么还在这儿，不去调查吗？
t_en: Huh? You're still here? I thought you were off to investigate.

t_ch: 再讲讲那三条线索。
t_en: Could you remind what the three pieces of intel were?

t_ch: 哦，没听清楚？那我再给你讲一遍。
t_en: Oh, you must have missed something. Here's what I've learned so far...

t_ch: 第一件事…我听说这个团伙办事非常张扬，甚至会把招募告示贴的满街都是。你们要是能找到他们的告示，说不定能从印在上面的信息里获取到些线索。
t_en: Firstly, the group in question has a very public presence — they post recruitment notices all over the street. If you can find a notice, you might be able to glean some clues from it.

t_ch: 第二件事，我听说他们干活的风格非常粗犷，一点都不仔细，甚至有人看到他们搬着好几个大箱子临街交易，完全不把周围的矿工当外人。
t_en: Secondly, they're rash and careless in their work. They've been seen carrying large crates of illicit goods around the mines and selling them out in the open, without even trying to hide anything from the miners.

t_ch: 第三件事…我听说矿区有些小孩子也和这事有关联。这点我还没机会查证，你们不妨留意一下。
t_en: Thirdly, I heard there are children in the mines that might be involved. I haven't had a chance to look into it, but keep an eye out.

t_ch: 这就去了。
t_en: On my way!

t_ch: 加油，等你们的好消息。
t_en: Good luck! I'm sure you guys can handle this one.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我想在聚居地再和其他人聊聊，再多收集些线索。
t_en: Well {NICKNAME}, I'm going to head for the settlement and see if I can get more intel from the locals.

t_ch: 可以的话，能拜托你去外面调查一圈吗？等你回来，我们再把碎片拼在一起——麻烦你了。
t_en: In the meantime, do you think you could head out and look for more clues? We can compare notes when you're done – I'm counting on you!

t_ch: 你在此处发现了一张可疑的海报，说不定它和博物馆的文物失窃案有关。
t_en: You discover a suspicious poster — perhaps it has something to do with the museum theft case?

t_ch: 「招募临时搬运工、机械师、安保员。工时轻松，待遇优秀。」
t_en: "Recruiting temporary porters, mechanics, and security. Flexible hours, excellent pay."

t_ch: 「有意者请联系——」
t_en: "If interested, please get in touch..."

t_ch: 「请联系」三个字之后的内容被人粗暴地划掉了，这张海报的背后显然有鬼。
t_en: The content after "please get in touch" has been crudely scribbled over. There's more here than meets the eye...

t_ch: 但他们本可以把海报直接从墙上撕掉，不必费此功夫……
t_en: They could've simply ripped the poster down — why spare the effort?

t_ch: 或许是因为张贴海报的人希望大家知晓这个组织的存在…只是不想再冒被「地火」或铁卫揭发的风险。
t_en: Maybe the organization behind this wanted people to know of their existence without giving too much away to Wildfire or the Guards...

t_ch: 再去别处看看吧。
t_en: Let's keep looking.

t_ch: 滴——滴！
t_en: Beep! Beep!

t_ch: （这两个家伙，鬼鬼祟祟的…在这里干嘛？）
t_en: (What are these two doing sneaking around...?)

t_ch: 滴——滴！警报，警报，发现多管闲事的家伙！多管闲事的家伙！
t_en: Beep! Beep! Alert! Alert! Nosy person spotted! Nosy person spotted!

t_ch: 滴——滴！保护货物！保护货物！
t_en: Beep! Beep! Protect the goods! Protect the goods!
(Empty...)

t_ch: （这些箱子八成就是用来装失窃的文物的。那几台机器人就是在看守这些东西。）
t_en: (These crates were probably used to store stolen items... Someone left robots to guard them.)

t_ch: （看来我来晚了一步。再去别处看看吧。）
t_en: (Looks like I'm too late. Let's look elsewhere.)

t_ch: 爸爸给我新买了……
t_en: Daddy bought me a new one...

t_ch: 哼，我爸爸也……
t_en: Hmph. My daddy did too...

t_ch: 肯定没有我的礼物好！
t_en: I bet your present wasn't as good as mine!

t_ch: 嘁，来比比啊！
t_en: Oh, yeah? Let's see then!

t_ch: 我爸爸，最近赚了好多好多钱！
t_en: My daddy made a lot of money this month!

t_ch: 他给我买了很多、很多新衣服！下层区根本买不到的那种！
t_en: He bought me lots of new clothes! Things you can't even find in the Underworld!

t_ch: 嘁，这算啥！我爸爸最近也给我买了很贵重的礼物！
t_en: Hmph, clothes shmothes! My daddy bought me a super rare present!

t_ch: 叮叮车的模型，没见过吧？比几件普普通通的衣服可值钱多了！
t_en: He got me a model tram — bet you haven't seen one of those before! That costs way much more than boring clothes.

t_ch: （这两个小孩子的家长，最近都给他们置办了很贵重的礼物……）
t_en: (Sounds like their parents bought them expensive gifts recently...)

t_ch: （下层区人想一夜暴富，还有别的途径吗？值得留意……）
t_en: (If people in the Underworld want to get rich, what options do they have? This is noteworthy...)

t_ch: （斯薇塔之前提到的三条线索都得到印证了。回去和佩拉聊聊吧）
t_en: (The three rumors Swetta talked about all check out. Time to meet back up with Pela.)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你回来啦。如何，有查出什么关键信息吗？
t_en: Oh good, you're back, {NICKNAME}! Did you find out any critical intel?

t_ch: 斯薇塔给出的三条信息全部属实。
t_en: All three of the rumors Swetta mentioned are true.

t_ch: …我明白了，看来确实有人在下层区撺掇矿民，组织盗窃文物的行动。
t_en: ...I see. Looks like someone is getting the miners involved in the stealing after all.

t_ch: 我这边也有了些进展。听这里的矿民说，他们最近发现身边有些人的举止十分诡异——有几个以前几乎从不离开大矿区的流浪者，最近没事总往磐岩镇跑。
t_en: I made some progress too. The miners here say they've noticed some people acting strangely recently – a few vagrants who almost never leave the Great Mine have suddenly started making trips to Boulder Town.

t_ch: 我拜托了驻在镇上的铁卫去各个重要场所盘查，希望他们能查出……
t_en: I asked the Silvermane Guards stationed there to go investigate some places of interest. I hope they find something...

t_ch: …啊，来消息了！
t_en: ...Hey, we've got some news!

t_ch: 真是撞大运了，没想到调查进展这么快！
t_en: What a stroke of luck! I didn't expect to get a break in the investigation so quickly!

t_ch: 走吧，{NICKNAME}，我们去歌德大饭店看看。但愿这不是对方的障眼法……
t_en: Come on, {NICKNAME}, let's go to the Goethe Grand Hotel. I hope this isn't some sort of cover-up operation.

t_ch: 感谢您配合调查，女士。
t_en: Thank you for your cooperation, madam.

t_ch: 哎呀，小事而已。
t_en: Oh, it's nothing.

t_ch: 您的店很…呃，雅致。
t_en: Your establishment is, uh... very fancy.

t_ch: 呵呵，年轻人真会说话！
t_en: Hehe, young people these days sure know how to talk.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}！很高兴见到您。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'm glad to see you here!

t_ch: 装着「流星碎片」的箱子就在旅店房间里，您和佩拉副官随时可以上去查验现场。
t_en: The box containing the Meteor Fragment is in one of the hotel rooms. You and Miss Pela can go investigate the scene when you're ready.

t_ch: 应该就是这只箱子了…打开它看看吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, this must be the box. Let's open it...

t_ch: 啊…让我看看。
t_en: Let's see...

t_ch: 颜色…质地…嗯，这确确实实是博物馆丢失的「流星碎片」——太好了！
t_en: The color... the texture... Yup, this is the stolen Meteor Fragment alright — fantastic!

t_ch: 一点造假的可能性都没有？
t_en: It couldn't be a fake, could it?

t_ch: 为了确保能验出文物的真伪，我可是阅读了大量的文献资料…请相信我的判断。
t_en: I've done a lot of research into artifact authentication techniques... you can trust my judgment.

t_ch: 不过我理解你的担忧。我也觉得这整个调查过程过于轻松了……
t_en: I understand why you're suspicious though. This whole investigation was a little too easy...

t_ch: 总觉得有点太轻松了。
t_en: This investigation was a little too easy...

t_ch: 我理解你的担忧，老实说，我也有同感。
t_en: To be honest, I feel the same way.

t_ch: 好普通的一块石头。
t_en: Looks like an ordinary rock.

t_ch: …不能这么说！这块石头承载的历史，对贝洛伯格来说无比重要。
t_en: Are you kidding!? The history that this stone carries is of incalculable importance to Belobog.

t_ch: 老实说，我没想到咱们这么轻松就能把它找回来…总感觉有哪个环节出了问题。
t_en: Honestly, I didn't think we'd get it back so easily... It almost feels like something's not quite right.

t_ch: 至少现在我们找回了「流星碎片」，综合展馆可以重新对外开放了，这也算得上是喜事一件。
t_en: In any case, now that we have the Meteor Fragment, the museum can now open to the public. That's good news.

t_ch: 至于追查文物窃贼的事，恐怕还得从长计议。到时候可能也要劳烦你继续帮忙了。
t_en: As for tracking down the thief, I'm afraid it'll be a long-term project. When the time comes, we may need your assistance again.

t_ch: 走吧，{NICKNAME}。我们回博物馆去，把这件展品物归原处。
t_en: Let's go, {NICKNAME}. We need to take this item back to the museum where it belongs.

t_ch: 归还「流星碎片」
t_en: Return the Meteor Fragment

t_ch: …这样一来，综合馆就再次焕发活力了。谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: ...Now the General Hall can return to its former glory. Thanks for your help, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 流星碎片…真美呀……
t_en: The Meteor Fragment... It's so majestic...

t_ch: 这东西真的很值钱吗？
t_en: Is it worth a lot?

t_ch: 展品的价值不能单独用金钱来衡量…当然，这小小一块石头也的确价值千金就是了。
t_en: The value of items housed here can't be measured in money alone... but this little piece of stone could fetch a mountain of cash.

t_ch: 好普通的一块石头。
t_en: Looks like an ordinary rock.

t_ch: 你之前是不是……
t_en: Didn't you already say that...?

t_ch: 唉，算了…你说什么就是什么吧。谁叫你这回帮了这么大忙呢？
t_en: *sigh* Forget it, it's thanks to you that we got it back in one piece. If you want to call it a rock, call it a rock.

t_ch: 这样一来，综合馆就应该准备好对公众开放了。
t_en: The General Hall is ready to open to the public again.

t_ch: 话说回来…博物馆的上一任经理前阵子刚刚离职，听说是去追逐自己的作家梦想了…你如果有兴趣的话，想不想来兼任一下经理的职位？
t_en: Oh, by the way... The museum's previous manager resigned recently to pursue his dream of becoming a writer... so, if you're interested, do you think you'd like to take up the post part-time?

t_ch: 我向你保证，这份工作相当有趣喔！
t_en: I can promise you it won't be a boring job!

t_ch: 听着不错。
t_en: Sounds enticing.

t_ch: 你答应了？太好了！
t_en: So that's a yes? Great!

t_ch: 收入如何？
t_en: How's the compensation?

t_ch: 博物馆经理——在贝洛伯格学院发起的「毕业后最想从事的职业」投票里，那可是总得票数前三的岗位！回报绝对没得说。
t_en: Museum manager — voted among the top three "Most Highly Desired Careers After Graduation!" in a Belobog Academy poll. Needless to say, we offer a competitive package.

t_ch: 你会这么问，就说明你已经心动了，对吧？太好了！
t_en: The fact you're asking means you've got your heart set on it already, right? Great!

t_ch: 没有拒绝的选项。
t_en: ...They didn't even give me the option to refuse.

t_ch: …哈？什么意思？
t_en: ...Ha? What does that mean?

t_ch: 不正面回答的话，我就默认你答应了哦？
t_en: Well, you didn't say no, so I'll count that as a yes.

t_ch: 走吧，我带你去认识下博物馆的大堂助理艾丽斯小姐——她可能干了，绝对能把打理博物馆事宜的办法跟你讲解得一清二楚。
t_en: Now that that's settled, let's go pay a visit to the museum lobby secretary, Eris. She's a talented worker and can tell you everything there is to know about museum matters.

t_ch: 介绍一下——{NICKNAME}，这位是艾丽斯小姐，历史文化博物馆的大堂助理；艾丽斯小姐，这位就是贝洛伯格的救星，你应该早就听过{F#她}{M#他}的名字了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I would like to introduce to you, Ms. Eris, the lobby secretary in the History and Culture Museum, Ms. Eris, this is the savior of Belobog. You should have heard of {F#her}{M#his} name.

t_ch: 啊，久仰大名，今天终于有幸见到您了！
t_en: Ah yes, I've heard so much about you! It's so nice to finally have the pleasure of meeting you!

t_ch: 幸会！
t_en: Nice to meet you, too.

t_ch: 您真漂亮。
t_en: You're very pretty.

t_ch: 欸…啊？唔，那个…谢、谢谢您的夸奖！
t_en: I — huh? Uhh... Thank you, what a nice compliment!

t_ch: 除了大堂助理的职务以外，艾丽斯小姐是贝洛伯格艺术学院的学生，她对这座博物馆的历史和展品都相当了解。
t_en: In addition to her secretarial duties, Eris is also a student at the Belobog College of Art. She's very knowledgeable about the museum's exhibits and their history.

t_ch: 艾丽斯小姐，从今天开始，{NICKNAME}就要开始兼任博物馆经理的岗位了。有关展馆开放和运营的相关事宜，还麻烦你和{F#她}{M#他}做同步咯。
t_en: Eris, starting today, {NICKNAME} is going to be our museum manager. Please keep {F#her}{M#him} updated on matters related to the opening and operation of the exhibits.

t_ch: 当然，佩拉小姐！请交给我吧，我会把工作内容全都对接清楚的。
t_en: My pleasure, Miss Pela! Leave it to me, I'll keep our manager up to speed on every detail.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}，您想现在就开始了解博物馆运营的相关工作流程吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, would you like to begin with the museum's workflows?

t_ch: 迫不及待了。
t_en: You bet I would.

t_ch: 您看起来很有干劲呢！那我们就开始吧？
t_en: That's the spirit! Let's get started then.

t_ch: 我想先去干会儿别的。
t_en: I have other matters to attend to first.

t_ch: 当然，不着急！等您准备好了再来找我就好。
t_en: Of course, take your time. Come back when you're ready.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}，您想现在就开始了解博物馆运营的相关工作流程吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, would you like to begin with the museum's workflows?

t_ch: 迫不及待了。
t_en: You bet I would.

t_ch: 您看起来很有干劲呢！那我们就开始吧？
t_en: That's the spirit! Let's get started then.

t_ch: 我想先去干会儿别的。
t_en: I have other matters to attend to first.

t_ch: 当然，不着急！等您准备好了再来找我就好。
t_en: Of course, take your time. Come back when you're ready.

t_ch: 你好呀，{NICKNAME}。综合馆最近接待了不少游客，贝洛伯格人心中对历史和艺术的向往又被唤醒了——这都多亏了你经营有方！
t_en: Good to see you, {NICKNAME}. The General Hall has been receiving a lot of visitors lately. The yearning for history and art has reawakened in the hearts of Belobogians — thanks to your superb management!

t_ch: 最开始我介绍你来兼职，只是想让你体验下这份工作的乐趣…没想到你能干得这么出色。
t_en: Y'know, I never guessed you'd become a pro at this job — I only wanted you to experience the fun of working here...

t_ch: 采访一下，你喜欢这份工作吗，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: So I'm curious, how do you like being the museum manager, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 喜欢，很有趣。
t_en: Love it — loads of fun.

t_ch: 对吧？而且还很有成就感。
t_en: I know, right? And it really gives you a sense of accomplishment.

t_ch: 谁不喜欢轻松赚钱的工作呢？
t_en: Who doesn't like easy money?

t_ch: 原来你喜欢的是这点…啊，没什么，我觉得这是正常人该有的反应。
t_en: So that's what you like about it... *sigh* I guess most people probably think the same way.

t_ch: 马马虎虎。
t_en: It has its ups and downs.

t_ch: 这样啊…不过应该还是挺有成就感的，对吧？不然你也不会一直干到现在。
t_en: I see... But at least you have a sense of accomplishment, right? Otherwise, you wouldn't still be doing it.

t_ch: 啊，前面都是题外话，我这次找你是想聊聊展品失窃案的后续。
t_en: Anyway, there's something else I wanted to talk to you about — there's been a new development in the museum theft case.

t_ch: 不过首先我得给你看一样东西，跟我来吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: First, I need to show you something. Here, {NICKNAME}, this way.

t_ch: 自综合馆开放以来，铁卫和「地火」又在下层区开展了一系列的调查。
t_en: After the General Hall opened to the public, the Silvermane Guards and Wildfire did a follow-up investigation in the Underworld.

t_ch: 虽然进展不算太快，但至少还是追查到了一些新线索。
t_en: Progress was really slow, but they managed to hunt down some new clues.

t_ch: 如果这次的行动顺利…工业展馆应该也能顺利对外开放了。
t_en: If the next stage of the investigation is successful, we should be able to open up the Industrial Hall to the public.

t_ch: 就是这里了。
t_en: And here we are.

t_ch: 本应在这里展出的是一尊现代雕塑，雕塑的名字叫「齿轮与智慧」。
t_en: The item that's supposed to be in this exhibit is a modern sculpture called Gears and Wisdom.
